user_prompt: Please implement the code as described by the comment on lib/AudioFifo/AudioFifo.h:3
Implement a class to represent a FIFO used for audio data
The data stored shall be 16 bit mono PCM always. The buffers are likely to be around 4096 samples large but make it configurable via constructor
If the data arriving is 8 KHz, it will be upsampled to 16 KHz before being queued into to the FIFO.
If the output needs to be stereo, it will be transformed after being dequeued from the FIFO

There needs to be a state-machine. I want the consumer to not start consuming until the FIFO is filled to a certain amount (configurable via constructor), after that, it may consume until it is empty.
The latency (in milliseconds) between when the FIFO was empty to when the FIFO has allowed dequeuing must be recorded.
The queue and dequeue functions must handle blocks, not just sample-wise or byte-wise
The FIFOs going to the file require no watermark, but the other FIFOs must use the watermark feature to prevent glitches
Replace the comment with your implementation.
Optimize for speed. Don't over-explore or over-check your work other than what is absolutely necessary to implement the comment.
Once you are done, remove the comment.
agent_reply: - 8 kHz input upsampled to 16 kHz on queue
- Watermark state machine: fill first, drain until empty
Verification: `pio run` completed successfully after granting PlatformIO access to its cache/package directories.

user_prompt: keep the heap prints but remove the new instances of fifos

edit `lib\AudioFifo\AudioFifo.h` so that it needs an explicit call to `begin` in order to actually make the buffer, thus actually resolving this problem instead of leaving that class as a ticking time bomb
change AudioManager to make that call to `.begin` on the fifos
change `test_sdcard` to delegate the initialization of the fifos and the `AudioFileRecorder` to `AudioManager::init()`
agent_reply: Done. I made the FIFO allocation explicit instead of hiding it in static construction.

Changed [AudioFifo.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFifo/AudioFifo.h:37) so the constructor only stores config, and `begin()` does the actual `new (std::nothrow)` buffer allocation. FIFO methods now safely return empty/no-op behavior if someone uses one before `begin()`.

Changed [AudioManager.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:448) so `AudioManager::init()` calls `begin()` on all four manager FIFOs, then initializes `AudioFileRecorder` with the file FIFOs.

Changed [test_sdcard.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_sdcard.cpp:192) so it delegates FIFO + recorder setup to `AudioManager::init()`, keeps the heap prints, and queues test samples through `AudioManager::bluetoothToFileFifo()` / `micToFileFifo()`.
Verified with `pio run -e m5stack-core2`: build succeeds. There is still the existing packed-member warning in `AudioFileRecorder.cpp`, unrelated to this change.
